---
title: "Interactive apps"
toc: true
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.kicker}
SHINY · INTERACTIVE COMPANIONS
:::
# Interactive apps
::: {.lead}
Twelve Shiny apps that let you turn the dials on the most important
ideas in the curriculum.
:::
:::

The apps complement the labs. Each lab teaches you the mechanics of a
method on a fixed dataset; the corresponding app lets you wiggle every
parameter and watch the answer change. Build intuition first, then
read the lab — or read the lab first and use the app to stress-test
your understanding.

::: {.callout-tip}
**Source:** every app is a single `app.R` in
[`shiny/<app-name>/`](https://github.com/r-heller/courses/tree/main/shiny)
on GitHub. They are designed for self-hosted Shiny Server but run
locally with one command:

```bash
R -e 'shiny::runApp("shiny/clt-explorer", launch.browser = TRUE)'
```
:::

The links below point at the deployed apps on the curriculum's Shiny
host. Replace `https://shiny.example.org` in `_quarto.yml` (or in this
file) with your own host once the server is up.

## Course 1 — Foundations

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 3 · SESSION 1
:::
### [CLT explorer](https://shiny.example.org/clt-explorer/)
Pick a parent distribution and a sample size; watch the sampling
distribution of the mean tighten and turn Gaussian as *n* grows.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 3 · SESSION 2
:::
### [Bootstrap CI explorer](https://shiny.example.org/bootstrap-ci/)
Pick a statistic, a population, n, and B. The app draws one sample,
resamples it B times, and gives a percentile CI you can compare to the
true value.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 2 · SESSION 3
:::
### [Diagnostic-test calculator](https://shiny.example.org/diagnostic-test/)
Sliders for sensitivity, specificity, and prevalence; outputs the
2×2 table, PPV/NPV, likelihood ratios, and the PPV-vs-prevalence curve.
:::

:::

## Course 2 — Regression

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 1 · SESSION 4
:::
### [Linear-model diagnostics](https://shiny.example.org/lm-diagnostics/)
Generate data with chosen slope and noise (and optional
heteroscedasticity / outliers); inspect the four diagnostic plots
side-by-side.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 3 · SESSIONS 1 & 5
:::
### [Logistic regression explorer](https://shiny.example.org/logistic-explorer/)
Pick intercept and slope; the app simulates, fits, and shows the
fitted curve, ROC, decile-binned calibration, and Brier score.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 2 · SESSION 1
:::
### [One-way ANOVA + contrasts](https://shiny.example.org/anova-contrasts/)
Pick the number of groups and within-group noise; the app shows the
boxplot, ANOVA table, and Tukey HSD pairwise comparisons.
:::

:::

## Course 3 — Design & Causal

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 3 · SESSION 3
:::
### [DAG explorer](https://shiny.example.org/dag-explorer/)
Pick a pre-built DAG (confounder, mediator, collider, M-bias) or paste
your own. The app computes minimal sufficient adjustment sets via
`dagitty`.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 2 · SESSION 1
:::
### [Missing-data simulator](https://shiny.example.org/missing-data-sim/)
Compare the bias of complete-case analysis under MCAR, MAR, and
MNAR mechanisms over many replications.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 4 · SESSION 4
:::
### [SIR / SEIR simulator](https://shiny.example.org/sir-simulator/)
Pick R0, infectious period, population size, and initial infected.
The app integrates the ODEs and reports peak prevalence and final
attack rate.
:::

:::

## Course 4 — ML & High-dimensional

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 1 · SESSION 1
:::
### [CV vs nested CV](https://shiny.example.org/cv-vs-nested/)
With pure noise data, naive CV looks confident but is wrong; nested
CV recovers the honest MSE. A 30-second illustration of the
selection-bias trap.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 1 · SESSION 2
:::
### [Regularisation path](https://shiny.example.org/regularization-path/)
Pick n, p, sparsity, SNR, and elastic-net alpha; see the full
coefficient path with the truly non-zero predictors highlighted, and
the CV curve with `lambda.min` and `lambda.1se` marked.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
WEEK 3 · SESSION 5
:::
### [Decision curves](https://shiny.example.org/decision-curves/)
Pick prevalence and the AUC of two prediction models; compare net
benefit across thresholds against treat-all and treat-none.
:::

:::

## Deploying to your Shiny Server

The apps assume a stock self-hosted Shiny Server with each app folder
served at a route matching its name. To deploy them all:

```bash
rsync -avz --exclude README.md shiny/ user@your-host:/srv/shiny-server/
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

To install the R packages they depend on, on the server:

```r
install.packages(c(
  "shiny", "ggplot2", "dplyr", "tidyr", "broom",
  "dagitty", "ggdag", "deSolve", "glmnet", "pROC", "MASS"
))
```

Once your apps are live, edit the URLs above (or use a global
`{{< var shiny_host >}}` variable in `_quarto.yml`) so they point at
your host instead of the placeholder.

## Embedding an app inside a lab

To embed an app on a lab page rather than link to it, drop in an
iframe:

```html
<iframe src="https://shiny.example.org/clt-explorer/"
        width="100%" height="650" frameborder="0">
</iframe>
```

This works in any lab `.qmd` and renders identically in both the HTML
article and the Reveal.js deck.
